text: During the laparoscopic appendectomy, pus is visible in the right paracolic gutter and pelvis. The appendix is amalgamated with the terminal ileum and cecum, forming an appendicular mass. Blunt dissection is performed to separate the appendix from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon uses an aspirator to perform blunt dissection of the appendix from the surrounding terminal ileum and cecum. The surgical field appears hyperemic. A Maryland Dissector is employed with electrocautery to dissect the mesoappendix, followed by the use of a hook due to the toughness of the mesoappendix tissue.

The surgeon performs blunt dissection of the hyperemic and bloody tissues surrounding the appendix, using a Maryland dissector to locate and separate the base of the appendix from adjacent structures.
The surgeon uses the Melanin Dissector to perform further blunt dissection, aiming to reach the proper base of the appendix, with visible manipulation of the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon uses a grasper to enter the loop and securely hold the appendix, followed by a note pusher to slide the knot around the base of the appendix.
The surgeon cuts the thread at the proper base of the appendix, preparing to sever the appendix from its attachment.
The surgeon retrieves the appendix through the umbilical port using a 5mm laparoscopic lens, followed by irrigation and aspiration of the surgical field before inserting a drain.
